message:
feat: replace regex IP validation with RFC-compliant parser in node:net shim, and sort FindTool results by modification time

node:net IP address validation rewrite (extensions_js.rs):
- Replaced the regex-based isIP/isIPv4/isIPv6 implementation with proper
  validation functions. The previous regex (/^(\d{1,3}\.){3}\d{1,3}$/)
  accepted invalid addresses like 999.999.999.999 and 256.0.0.1 because
  it only checked digit counts, not octet value ranges.
- New __pi_net_is_ipv4(): splits on '.', validates exactly 4 parts, each
  must be a 1-3 digit number in the 0-255 range.
- New __pi_net_is_ipv6(): handles '::' abbreviation (at most one),
  validates each segment as 1-4 hex digits, supports dual-stack
  IPv4-mapped addresses (e.g., ::ffff:192.0.2.1) by delegating the
  trailing IPv4 portion to __pi_net_is_ipv4, and enforces the total
  segment count <= 8.
- __pi_net_ipv6_segment_count() helper counts segments in a split half,
  treating IPv4-mapped segments as 2 segments per RFC 4291.
- isIPv4/isIPv6 now call the dedicated helpers directly instead of
  routing through isIP, avoiding redundant checks.

FindTool sort-by-mtime (tools.rs):
- FindTool results are now sorted by modification time (newest first)
  instead of case-insensitive alphabetical order. This matches the
  behavior users expect when searching for recently-changed files and
  aligns with how Glob results are presented elsewhere.
- Added FindEntry struct carrying both the relative path and an optional
  SystemTime from fs::metadata().modified(). Files without readable mtime
  sort after those with one.
- Fallback sort within the same mtime bucket remains case-insensitive
  alphabetical for determinism.
- Updated tool description to document the new sort order.

Test:
- net_isip_validation: comprehensive test covering IPv4 valid (127.0.0.1,
  1.2.3.4, 255.255.255.255), IPv4 invalid (256.x.x.x per octet), IPv6
  valid (full and :: abbreviated), and IPv6 invalid (double ::).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
